title: RNA extraction from colonial tunicates
author: Marta Wawrzyniak
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b33nqqme

steps.title: Clean the slide from which you...
steps.instruction: Clean the slide from which you will take the colony of your interest. See Cleaning colonial ascidians .

steps.title: Isolate a cleaned colony composed of approx
steps.instruction: Isolate a cleaned colony composed of approx. 20 zooids.

steps.title: Transfer to a tube and spin...
steps.instruction: Transfer to a tube and spin at maximum speed for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Remove the excess water and shock-freeze...
steps.instruction: Remove the excess water and shock-freeze the tube in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of extraction buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of extraction buffer to the frozen sample and macerate with a plastic pestle.

steps.title: Add 100 µL more of extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL more of extraction buffer and 500 µL of 1:1 phenol:chloroform.

steps.title: Mix the tube by inversion a...
steps.instruction: Mix the tube by inversion a couple of times until it gets cloudy.

steps.title: Centrifuge the homogenate at 1400 g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the homogenate at 1400 g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Carefully collect 400 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully collect 400 µL of the upper phase into a new tube.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: if desired this sample could be used for DNA extraction - carefully transfer 200 µL of the interphase into a new tube (See DNA extraction from colonial tunicates ).

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 6 Molarity...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 6 Molarity (M) LiCl to the supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture at -80 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture at -80 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:10:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 3 Molarity (M) LiCl.

steps.title: Shake slowly for 00:15:00 at Room...
steps.instruction: Shake slowly for 00:15:00 at Room temperature on a linear shaker.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:10:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of SC-EtOH solution.

steps.title: Incubate at -80 °C for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at -80 °C for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:15:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:15:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash the pellet with 1 mL of 70 % volume Ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1400 g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and place the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and place the tubes up-side-down on a paper towel for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in RNase-free water...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in RNase-free water ( 20 µL to 100 µL depends on the amount of pellet).

steps.title: Store at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store at -80 °C .

steps.title: Quantify the RNA concentration and quality...
steps.instruction: Quantify the RNA concentration and quality using the NanoDrop, the capillary electrophoresis and/or the Bioanalyzer.
